title: Xylothamia palmeri
taxonomy: Xylothamia palmeri is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Xylothamia palmeri is within the genus Xylothamia, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as a subgenus of the genus Hymenothrix. The plant is further classified as a member of the family Asteraceae and order Asterales. The classification of Xylothamia palmeri is still subject to ongoing research and possible revisions in botanical classification systems.

intro: Xylothamia palmeri, also known as Palmer's xylothamia, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Xylothamia palmeri include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Xylothamia palmeri is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
